posts.text: Two oil tankers and two LPG carriers are crossing the Strait of Hormuz **inbound** via the Omani route with their AIS turned on (under heavy US Navy / Airforce watch). Washington trying hard to keep the Omani route on.
posts.summary:
# Oil Market Summary

The U.S. is actively monitoring and facilitating tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz via the Omani route to maintain crude oil supply flows, suggesting efforts to stabilize energy markets amid regional tensions. Four vessels (two oil tankers and two LPG carriers) are currently transiting with active tracking, indicating coordinated efforts to keep critical shipping lanes operational.

posts.text:
Rystad Energy sees Middle East oil flows fully recovering before the end of the year.

Why? 

1. Most large oil production and export assets were left unscathed. 

2. After months of lost income, Middle East oil producers are now more incentivized than ever to ramp oil production
posts.summary: Middle East oil production is expected to return to normal levels by year-end due to intact infrastructure and strong economic incentives for producers to increase output. This recovery could help stabilize global oil supplies that were disrupted by recent production losses in the region.
